question_frequency.rationale: The Pair never asks the user a single question. It presents one solution with explanation and the user accepts it. The didactic two-option format described in the project's CLAUDE.md is entirely absent.

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all — no A/B options, no fork on the classic gotcha (century vs 400-year rule), no trade-off discussion. The most teachable moment in this exercise (why the century exception works) was simply explained, not interrogated.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no sequence of questions to scaffold. The Pair delivered the full answer immediately, including a truth table and worked examples, leaving no room for progressive discovery.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and the explanation is well-organized with a helpful table and sanity checks. But it's a one-shot lecture rather than a back-and-forth, so conversational flow is minimal.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
